title: pkcs11 put: stale-read possible after partial failure, doc says 'data loss not possible' but omits indeterminate-read
description: If put() succeeds at create_object but fails at destroy_object, two objects with the same label exist. The next get() calls find_objects which returns all matches; find_object takes the first (PKCS#11 spec does not define order). The caller receives SecretError::Backend from put(), then calls get() expecting the new value, but may receive the old value. The atomicity doc correctly says 'data loss is not possible' but does not say 'reads are indeterminate until next successful put'. Users will file: I called put(), got an error, called get(), still got the old value.
